title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today I am with my husband who is Korean and he's going to complete some missions here in Ite1 to see if he can practice his English without ever leaving the country.
I'm going to stay at the table while Hong goes up to order by himself and asks his three questions.
We are going to Cali Kitchen, which is a popular American bar and restaurant here.
I'm scared to see what condition you're going to be in.
I'm going to pick you up.

title: will / shall
examples: Maybe by the end of the day, you'll get more confident.
Okay, maybe I'll choose one and can you choose one for me then?
Yeah, I'll choose the blueberry almond crumble.
I will recommend anything with carnitas because most carnitas are good.
I'll have that.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: Maybe a tip will be trying to find their interest.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: How can I improve my English speaking?
So you can come here and practice your English speaking.
Today I am with my husband who is Korean and he's going to complete some missions here in Ite1 to see if he can practice his English without ever leaving the country.
Can I order?
Okay, maybe I'll choose one and can you choose one for me then?

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Eventually, at the bar, I made good friends, so I might go back to bar.

title: have to / must
examples: Luckily, you don't have to go to the US or the UK to practice your English speaking.
First mission is to order coffee and you must ask three questions while ordering.
You have to just approach some other customers and make a friend.
You have to see.
Yeah, I don't think I have to ask that question.

title: should (advice)
examples: You should drink it without thinking.
I should do you have any like, Instagram or something then?

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I would like to have a pumpkin spice latte.
And I would like to have a teaser.
I would have a lot of tips with macaramoli.
Would you mind if we get a box or they take out or something?
What would you recommend out of this?

title: Can/Could/Would you...?
examples: Can I order?
Would you mind if we get a box or they take out or something?
Always can you recommend?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: Would you mind if we get a box or they take out or something?

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: A lot of people here are open to make a new friend.
So for example, Ryan was interested in UFC and then a cooking show.

title: have something done
examples: We just got a table at Plant Cafe.
I've got a good restaurant.
